Fix code runner: Pyodide on main thread with prompt() for input()

Root cause: The previous approach tried to override builtins.input() with an
async Python function that used postMessage to communicate with the React
panel. This fundamentally cannot work because:
1. JsProxy objects don't support item assignment (the immediate error)
2. Even if fixed, async input() can't work inside synchronous Python
   functions (like a blackjack game's play loop) — only top-level await
   is supported by runPythonAsync

Fix: Load Pyodide directly in the main thread (not in a hidden iframe)
and patch input() with window.prompt() — a synchronous browser dialog.
This works for ALL code patterns: loops, nested functions, games, etc.

Changes:
- Python: Pyodide loaded via <script> tag, cached after first load
  - input() → window.prompt() (synchronous, works everywhere)
  - Auto-installs imported packages (numpy, pandas, scipy, matplotlib…)
  - Matplotlib figures captured as PNG images
  - 60s timeout
- Java: Auto-strip 'public' from class declarations (Godbolt quirk)
- Removed all broken async input infrastructure (postMessage, InputReq,
  input_request/response/ack, stdout_chunk, etc.)
- Verified all Godbolt compilers work: C++(g152), C(cg152), Go(gl1260),
  Rust(r1940), Java(java2501), Kotlin(kotlinc2220), Swift(swift62),
  Haskell(ghc9122), Ruby(ruby347)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: eliminate Stopwatch allocation from route dispatch hot path

Replace Stopwatch.StartNew() (object allocation + 2 TSC reads) with
Stopwatch.GetTimestamp() + GetElapsedTime() (stack-only, 1 TSC read
at start, 1 at exit).

On older Intel CPUs without invariant TSC, each Stopwatch call can
cost 100-1000+ cycles due to RDMSR kernel-mode serialization. The
old code had 14 separate .Stop()/.Elapsed call sites — each one a
pipeline-flushing serializing instruction on pre-Nehalem/Atom cores.

The new code:
- Single GetTimestamp() at dispatch entry (1 TSC read)
- Single GetElapsedTime() at each exit point (1 TSC read)
- No Stopwatch object allocation (saves GC pressure)
- No .Stop() calls (avoids redundant state transitions)

Expected improvement: 2-5ms on older Intel without invariant TSC.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Prometheus-compatible /metrics/prometheus endpoint (#1257)

Expose all MetricsTracker and EngineMetrics data in Prometheus text
exposition format (v0.0.4) at GET /metrics/prometheus.

Metrics exposed:
- HTTP request counters by status class (2xx/4xx/5xx/other/throttled)
- Request duration summary (P50/P95/P99)
- Process gauges (uptime, working set, virtual memory)
- GC collections by generation + total allocated bytes
- Subsystem counters (route dispatch, WAL read, UI render, serialization)
- WAL engine metrics (appends, locks, commits, compaction, replay)
- Delta size and commit retry counters

Endpoint is public (no auth) to allow Prometheus scraper access.
Existing /metrics (HTML admin view) and /metrics/json unchanged.

feat: server-side message queue survives client disconnects

Messages are now queued server-side per session. When a user sends
a message from their phone and switches apps (dropping WebSocket),
the message is already enqueued and will be delivered when the
current turn completes.

Architecture:
- Each session gets a buffered channel (capacity 100) as its queue
- drainQueue goroutine runs on server context (not client context),
  processing messages sequentially via SendPrompt
- Messages acknowledged immediately, processed asynchronously
- Queue workers survive client disconnects

commit.message:
feat: add post-processing shader pipeline with 4 presets

Add a multi-pass post-processing pipeline that renders the fractal to an
intermediate texture, then applies configurable GPU effects before
compositing to the canvas.

Effects implemented:
- Bloom: brightness extraction + separable Gaussian blur (half-res)
- Vignette: radial edge darkening with configurable softness
- Adaptive sharpening: Laplacian kernel scaled by local contrast
- Chromatic aberration: radial RGB channel offset
- ACES filmic tone mapping with exposure, saturation, and temperature

Presets (cycle with P / Shift+P):
- Clean: no effects (zero overhead, direct-to-canvas rendering)
- Cinematic: bloom + vignette + ACES tone mapping
- Vivid: bloom + sharpening + high saturation
- Dreamy: heavy bloom + chromatic aberration + vignette

New files:
- src/renderer/postprocess/ (pipeline manager, state, presets)
- src/renderer/shaders/bloom-extract.wgsl
- src/renderer/shaders/blur.wgsl
- src/renderer/shaders/composite.wgsl
- docs/post-processing-plan.md

Fix Python input() — fully interactive programs now work in the runner

The OSError [Errno 29] I/O error happened because Pyodide has no stdin.
Fix: override builtins.input() in the Pyodide iframe with an async version
that messages the parent and pauses Python execution until the user responds.

How it works:
- Python calls input('Hit or Stand? ')
- Pyodide sends input_request to the React panel
- Panel flushes the stdout accumulated since last input (so you see the
  game state before the prompt) then shows an input text field
- User types and presses Enter (or clicks the send button)
- Response is sent back to the iframe, Python resumes with the value
- Repeat for every subsequent input() call
- When execution ends the remaining stdout + plots are shown

Blackjack, text adventures, guessing games, any input()-driven program
will now run interactively end-to-end.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: switch to default branch before pulling after add-wizard PR merge (#20094)

* Initial plan

* fix: pull changes to default branch after PR merge in add-wizard (#issue)

After `gh aw add-wizard` merges the PR, `updateLocalBranch()` now
switches to the default branch before pulling, ensuring the working
tree contains the merged workflow files. Previously, if the user was on
a feature branch, `git pull origin <defaultBranch>` would merge the
remote branch into the wrong local branch and the workflow files would
not be in the working tree, causing "workflow file not found" when
trying to run immediately after setup.

Also surface the pull failure warning to users in non-verbose mode
with a hint to run `git pull` manually.

commit.message:
Fix infinite cron trigger fires during DST fall-back transition (#2865)

* Initial plan

* Fix infinite trigger fires during DST fall-back transition

When computing next fire time in CronExpression.GetTimeAfter, ambiguous
local times during DST fall-back transitions were assigned the DST
(summer) UTC offset, resulting in a UTC time that was before the input.
This caused triggers to always compute a next fire time in the past,
leading to infinite refires.

Fix: after computing the UTC result, if it falls before afterTimeUtc
and the local time is ambiguous, use the standard (non-DST) offset
instead — the minimum of the two ambiguous offsets — which corresponds
to the second (post-fallback) occurrence of the ambiguous local time.

Adds regression test ShouldNotFireInfinitelyAfterDstFallBackTransition
reproducing the Amsterdam/Europe scenario from the issue report.

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve medium findings C2-RR-4/6/7/8 and C2-RD-10/13

C2-RR-4: execute_with_depth now uses full step-execution logic matching
execute() — parallel groups, hooks, tag filtering, audit logging, and
listener notifications are all honored in sub-recipe execution.

C2-RR-6: Condition tokenizer now accepts hyphens in identifiers
(e.g. my-var) when the hyphen is followed by a letter. Hyphens
followed by digits are still treated as minus/negative-number tokens.

C2-RR-7: Dot-notation property access now works in conditions. When
ident.field is encountered and field is NOT a safe method name (or has
no parentheses), it is treated as property access into the context,
matching template rendering behavior. Dunder properties are still
blocked.

C2-RR-8: parse_and() and parse_or() now short-circuit — false AND x
returns false without evaluating x, true OR x returns true without
evaluating x.

C2-RD-10: Added test for timeout:0 edge case — verifies the step
executes and completes normally.

C2-RD-13: Bulk dry-run test now asserts tutorial recipes have non-empty
step results and matching recipe names.

commit.message:
Make tab navigation instant — skip skeletons when data is cached

Loading screens now only show on first app load. Once stores have data,
switching between tabs renders instantly with no skeleton flash — feels
like a native app.

Changes:
- All loading.tsx files are now 'use client' and check store state
  before showing skeletons (skipLoad: true avoids triggering fetches)
- Layout prefetches all tab route JS chunks via router.prefetch()
- Stores continue to prefetch data in the layout as before

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: interrupt/queue choice when sending while LLM is working

When the user sends a message while the LLM is processing, instead
of silently queuing it, a choice bar appears:

- ⏳ Queue: sends the message, shows 'Queued...' pending badge,
  delivered after the current turn completes
- ⚡ Interrupt: aborts the current turn via SDK Abort(), then
  sends the message immediately
- Cancel: dismisses the choice, keeps the draft

Also fixes:
- Pending messages survive history refreshes (turn_end no longer
  wipes them; re-added after re-render if not yet in history)
- pendingPrompt cleared when user_message broadcast confirms delivery

Backend:
- Session.Abort: wraps SDK session.Abort
- Manager.AbortSession: finds session by name and aborts
- Bridge: handles abort_session WS message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: OTP login and password reset flows (#90)

## Summary

Adds the missing OTP login UI to the mobile app. Users can now tap
"Login with email code" on the sign-in screen, enter their credentials,
and complete login with a 6-digit code sent to their Innopolis email.

## Changes

- `data/paths.dart` — added `/auth/login/otp/request` and
`/auth/login/otp/verify`
- `data/auth/auth_gateway.dart` + `auth_gateway_impl.dart` —
`loginOtpRequest` / `loginOtpVerify`
- `application/repositories/auth/otp_login_repository.dart` +
`_impl.dart` — stores session_token between steps
- `presentation/blocs/otp_login/otp_login_cubit.dart` +
`models/otp_login_state.dart`
- `presentation/pages/auth/subpages/otp_request_sub_page.dart` — Step 1:
email + password
- `presentation/pages/auth/subpages/otp_verify_sub_page.dart` — Step 2:
enter 6-digit code
- `presentation/router/app_router.dart` + `app_router.gr.dart` —
registered new routes
- `presentation/pages/auth/auth_page.dart` — wired `OtpLoginCubit`
provider
- `presentation/pages/auth/subpages/sign_in_sub_page.dart` — added
button
- `lib/app.dart` — registered `OtpLoginRepository` in DI

Closes #89
